creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
The original build log reference shows: "Installing rust-1.81.0"

Since then lang/rust 1.82.0 was committed and:

URL: https://cgit.FreeBSD.org/ports/commit/?id=560fd669d24aafad437789954800d57e7cfeafa9

commit 560fd669d24aafad437789954800d57e7cfeafa9
Author:     Mikael Urankar <mikael@FreeBSD.org>
AuthorDate: 2024-11-14 14:59:30 +0000
Commit:     Mikael Urankar <mikael@FreeBSD.org>
CommitDate: 2024-11-14 15:30:31 +0000

    lang/rust: Regen bootstrap for armv7

    PR:             282663

 lang/rust/distinfo | 14 +++++++-------
 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

was later committed. The result seems to allow building
lang/rust 1.82.0_1 on main [so: 15 as stands] --but not
on stable/14 or 1[34].*-RELEASE .

The "Regen" was to pick up for the bootstrap the fix for
what lead to the multiple definitions issue, avoiding
that of itself messing up building lang/rust (as least
for FreeBSD main).

Unfortunately, it could be more than 1 week for the
official package builder involved for FreeBSD main to
do an armv7 build and distribute it.

Once those are available, the "multiple definition"
problems should be gone for FreeBSD main. (No claim
made about other potential problems.)

So, other than "build delay", this might have been
fixed.


As for Comment #1 's "The failure only occurs on 15 for some reason.":

lang/rust has not built for armv7 for 1[34]*-RELEASE in at least
months. The records only go as far back as 2024-Jul-08 or so on:

https://portsfallout.com/fallout?port=lang%2Frust&maintainer=&env=&category=&flavor=

( which also shows lang/rustpython ) but armv7 lang/rust was
possibly not building before that as well.
